domain: E-Commerce Operations — Order Fulfillment, Seller Performance, Customer Satisfaction
source_files: olist_order_items_dataset.csv (Items per order — seller, product, price, freight)
description: Ontology for automated hypothesis generation over the Olist Brazilian E-Commerce dataset. Covers 9 interconnected tables enabling multi-hop traversal across Order, Customer, Seller, Product, Payment, Review, and Geographic dimensions. Primary analytical questions concern delivery delays, customer dissatisfaction, seller underperformance, and payment anomalies. No explicit fraud label — ground truth is derived via statistical methods (late delivery outliers, low review score patterns, payment installment anomalies).

datasource.description: Order lifecycle — status, timestamps, delivery performance

Order.OrderID: Text | Unique identifier for each order (order_id). Primary key linking all order-related tables.
Order.CustomerID: Text | Foreign key to Customer node (customer_id). Links the order to the purchasing customer.
Order.OrderStatus: Text | Current status of the order — delivered, shipped, canceled, unavailable, invoiced, processing, created, approved. 'delivered' is the terminal success state.
Order.OrderPurchaseTimestamp: DateTime | Timestamp when the customer placed the order.
Order.OrderApprovedAt: DateTime | Timestamp when the payment was approved by the platform.
Order.OrderDeliveredCarrierDate: DateTime | Timestamp when the order was handed to the logistics carrier.
Order.OrderDeliveredCustomerDate: DateTime | Timestamp when the order was actually delivered to the customer.
Order.OrderEstimatedDeliveryDate: Date | Estimated delivery date shown to the customer at purchase time.
Order.IsLate: Boolean | Derived — 1 if OrderDeliveredCustomerDate > OrderEstimatedDeliveryDate, 0 otherwise. Primary delivery performance signal.
Order.DaysLate: Numeric | Derived — number of days by which delivery exceeded estimate. Negative values = early delivery.
Order.ApprovalDelayHours: Numeric | Derived — hours between OrderPurchaseTimestamp and OrderApprovedAt. Long approval delays indicate payment processing issues.
Order.CarrierPickupDelayDays: Numeric | Derived — days between OrderApprovedAt and OrderDeliveredCarrierDate. Long gaps indicate seller preparation delays.
Order.TransitDays: Numeric | Derived — days between OrderDeliveredCarrierDate and OrderDeliveredCustomerDate. Reflects carrier performance.
Order.TotalOrderValue: Numeric | Derived — sum of all item prices + freight values for this order in BRL.

Customer.CustomerID: Text | Unique identifier per order (customer_id) — note: one physical customer can have multiple CustomerIDs across orders.
Customer.CustomerUniqueID: Text | Persistent unique identifier for the actual physical customer across all their orders.
Customer.CustomerCity: Text | City of the customer's delivery address.
Customer.CustomerState: Text | Brazilian state code (e.g., SP, RJ, MG) of the customer's delivery address.
Customer.CustomerZipCodePrefix: Text | 5-digit zip code prefix of the customer's delivery address.
Customer.Latitude: Numeric | Derived — latitude of customer's zip code from geolocation table.
Customer.Longitude: Numeric | Derived — longitude of customer's zip code from geolocation table.
Customer.TotalOrders: Numeric | Derived — total number of orders placed by this CustomerUniqueID.
Customer.AvgReviewScore: Numeric | Derived — average review score given by this customer across all their orders. Range 1-5.
Customer.LateDeliveryRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of this customer's orders that were delivered late.

Seller.SellerID: Text | Unique identifier for each seller on the Olist marketplace.
Seller.SellerCity: Text | City where the seller operates.
Seller.SellerState: Text | Brazilian state code where the seller operates.
Seller.SellerZipCodePrefix: Text | 5-digit zip code prefix of the seller's location.
Seller.Latitude: Numeric | Derived — latitude of seller's zip code from geolocation table.
Seller.Longitude: Numeric | Derived — longitude of seller's zip code from geolocation table.
Seller.TotalOrdersFulfilled: Numeric | Derived — total number of order items fulfilled by this seller.
Seller.AvgReviewScore: Numeric | Derived — average review score received by this seller across all orders. Range 1-5.
Seller.LateDeliveryRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of this seller's orders that were delivered late. Key seller performance KPI.
Seller.AvgCarrierPickupDelayDays: Numeric | Derived — average days between order approval and carrier pickup for this seller. High values indicate slow seller preparation.
Seller.CancellationRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of this seller's orders that were canceled.
Seller.AvgFreightValue: Numeric | Derived — average freight cost charged by this seller in BRL.
Seller.AvgItemPrice: Numeric | Derived — average item price for this seller's products in BRL.
Seller.SellerPerformanceScore: Numeric | Derived — composite score combining review score (normalized), late delivery rate (inverted), and cancellation rate (inverted). Range 0-1.

datasource.description: Line items within each order — price, freight, seller

OrderItem.OrderItemID: Numeric | Sequential item number within a multi-item order.
OrderItem.OrderID: Text | Foreign key to Order node.
OrderItem.ProductID: Text | Foreign key to Product node.
OrderItem.SellerID: Text | Foreign key to Seller node.
OrderItem.ShippingLimitDate: Date | Deadline by which the seller must ship this item to the carrier.
OrderItem.Price: Numeric | Item price in BRL as listed by the seller.
OrderItem.FreightValue: Numeric | Freight (shipping) cost for this item in BRL.
OrderItem.IsShippingLate: Boolean | Derived — 1 if OrderDeliveredCarrierDate > ShippingLimitDate, 0 otherwise. Seller-level shipping delay indicator.
OrderItem.PriceFreightRatio: Numeric | Derived — Price / FreightValue. Very low ratios (cheap items with high freight) may indicate price manipulation or geographic remote delivery.

Product.ProductID: Text | Unique identifier for each product listed on Olist.
Product.ProductCategoryNameEnglish: Text | Translated category name in English (from translation table).
Product.ProductNameLength: Numeric | Character count of the product name description.
Product.ProductDescriptionLength: Numeric | Character count of the product description.
Product.ProductPhotosQty: Numeric | Number of product photos provided by the seller. Low photo counts are associated with higher return/dissatisfaction rates.
Product.ProductWeightG: Numeric | Product weight in grams. Drives freight cost calculation.
Product.ProductVolumeCm3: Numeric | Derived — Length × Height × Width. Volumetric size proxy for freight cost.
Product.AvgReviewScore: Numeric | Derived — average review score for this product across all orders. Range 1-5.
Product.TotalUnitsSold: Numeric | Derived — total number of units of this product sold.
Product.LateDeliveryRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of orders containing this product that were delivered late.

datasource.description: Payment type, installments, value per order

Payment.OrderID: Text | Foreign key to Order node.
Payment.PaymentSequential: Numeric | Sequence number for multi-payment orders (e.g., combining voucher + credit card).
Payment.PaymentType: Text | Payment method — credit_card, boleto, voucher, debit_card.
Payment.PaymentInstallments: Numeric | Number of installments chosen by the customer. Range 1-24. High installment counts on high-value orders are normal in Brazil.
Payment.PaymentValue: Numeric | Value of this specific payment in BRL.
Payment.TotalPaymentValue: Numeric | Derived — sum of all payment sequences for this order. Should match TotalOrderValue.
Payment.PaymentValueDiscrepancy: Numeric | Derived — abs(TotalPaymentValue - TotalOrderValue). Non-zero values indicate payment anomalies.
Payment.IsVoucherUsed: Boolean | Derived — 1 if any payment sequence uses voucher type.
Payment.HighInstallmentFlag: Boolean | Derived — 1 if PaymentInstallments > 10 AND PaymentValue > 500 BRL. Indicates potential financial stress or installment abuse.

Review.ReviewID: Text | Unique identifier for each review submission.
Review.OrderID: Text | Foreign key to Order node.
Review.ReviewScore: Numeric | Customer satisfaction score. Range 1-5; 1=very dissatisfied, 5=very satisfied. Primary customer experience signal.
Review.ReviewCreationDate: DateTime | Timestamp when the review request was sent to the customer.
Review.ReviewAnswerTimestamp: DateTime | Timestamp when the customer submitted the review.
Review.ReviewResponseDays: Numeric | Derived — days between ReviewCreationDate and ReviewAnswerTimestamp. Long response times indicate disengaged customers.
Review.HasComment: Boolean | Derived — 1 if review_comment_message is not null, 0 otherwise.
Review.IsNegativeReview: Boolean | Derived — 1 if ReviewScore <= 2, 0 otherwise. Used as a dissatisfaction ground truth proxy.
Review.SentimentCategory: Text | Derived from ReviewScore — Negative (1-2), Neutral (3), Positive (4-5).

datasource.description: Zip code to lat/lng and distance calculations

GeographicLocation.ZipCodePrefix: Text | 5-digit Brazilian zip code prefix (CEP prefix).
GeographicLocation.Latitude: Numeric | Latitude coordinate for this zip code prefix.
GeographicLocation.Longitude: Numeric | Longitude coordinate for this zip code prefix.
GeographicLocation.City: Text | City name associated with this zip code.
GeographicLocation.SellerCustomerDistanceKm: Numeric | Derived — geodesic distance between seller and customer zip code centroids. Long distances correlate with late deliveries and high freight costs.
GeographicLocation.RegionName: Text | Derived — macro-region of Brazil: Norte, Nordeste, Centro-Oeste, Sudeste, Sul.

datasource.description: Pre-aggregated time-period delivery and review stats

TimeWindow.PurchaseDayOfWeek: Numeric | Day of week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday) of order purchase.
TimeWindow.PurchaseHour: Numeric | Hour (0-23) of order purchase timestamp.
TimeWindow.IsHolidaySeason: Boolean | Derived — 1 if month is November or December (Black Friday + Christmas peak). Associated with higher volumes and potential delivery degradation.
TimeWindow.AvgOrdersPerDay: Numeric | Derived — average daily order volume in this time period.
TimeWindow.AvgLateDeliveryRateInPeriod: Numeric | Derived — proportion of orders in this period that were delivered late.
TimeWindow.AvgReviewScoreInPeriod: Numeric | Derived — average review score for orders purchased in this period.

relationtrees.description: Directed paths through the 9-table Olist schema. Multi-hop traversals enable hypothesis generation about root causes of delivery failures, seller underperformance, and customer dissatisfaction.
multi_hop_example_paths: Order → Review → Seller → GeographicLocation (4 hops): Are low-review-score orders concentrated among sellers in specific Brazilian states — indicating regional logistics failures?
Order → OrderItem → Product → Category → Seller (5 hops): Are sellers in certain product categories consistently late — indicating category-specific fulfillment challenges?
Customer → Order → Payment → Installments → TimeWindow (5 hops): Do customers using high installment counts show higher cancellation rates during holiday seasons?
Seller → OrderItem → Order → Review → Customer (5 hops): Are specific sellers' late deliveries causing disproportionate review score drops — linking seller delay to customer churn risk?

GeographicLocation(Seller) → GeographicLocation(Customer) → Distance → TransitDays → LateDelivery (5 hops): Does geodesic seller-to-customer distance predict late delivery independently of seller performance?

axioms.name: Late delivery detection
axioms.rule: IF Order.OrderDeliveredCustomerDate > Order.OrderEstimatedDeliveryDate THEN Order.IsLate = TRUE AND Order.DaysLate = (OrderDeliveredCustomerDate - OrderEstimatedDeliveryDate).days

axioms.name: Seller shipping delay flag
axioms.rule: IF Seller.AvgCarrierPickupDelayDays > Q3(AvgCarrierPickupDelayDays) + 1.5 * IQR(AvgCarrierPickupDelayDays) THEN Seller.IsShippingSlowOutlier = TRUE

axioms.name: Review score delivery correlation
axioms.rule: IF Order.IsLate = TRUE AND Order.DaysLate > 7 THEN P(Review.ReviewScore <= 2) > 0.6

axioms.name: Distance-driven late delivery
axioms.rule: IF GeographicLocation.SellerCustomerDistanceKm > 1500 AND Seller.AvgCarrierPickupDelayDays < MEDIAN THEN Order.LateDeliveryRootCause = 'TransitDistance'

ground_truth_strategy.primary: Order.IsLate (derived from timestamp comparison) — deterministic delivery performance label
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_statistical: IQR outlier detection on Seller.LateDeliveryRate and DaysLate — identifies underperforming sellers
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_temporal: Rolling monthly z-score on AvgLateDeliveryRateInPeriod — flags seasonal degradation
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_geographic: Geodesic distance analysis — flags distance-driven delays vs seller-driven delays

hypothesis_examples: Sellers in the Norte and Nordeste regions of Brazil show disproportionately high late delivery rates because geodesic distance to São Paulo customers exceeds 2,000 km — the root cause is transit time, not seller preparation.

Orders placed during November and December show a 15-25% increase in late delivery rate because carrier capacity is overwhelmed during peak holiday season — the delay accumulates at the transit stage, not the seller stage.

Low-review-score orders (score 1-2) for the 'cama_mesa_banho' (bedding) category are disproportionately caused by product dimension mismatches with freight cost — customers report receiving damaged items due to inadequate packaging.
Sellers who take more than 5 days on average to hand orders to carriers have a late delivery rate 3× higher than the platform median, regardless of delivery distance.
Customers using 12+ payment installments on orders above 500 BRL show a cancellation rate 40% higher than single-installment customers — indicating financial stress-driven abandonment.
